EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A cold easterly wind through the Columbia River Gorge was keeping cold air trapped in the Gorge as a strong Pacific frontal system moved inland. This system spread precipitation over the Gorge and Upper Hood River Valley, starting as snow and changing over to freezing rain in the Gorge as the air mass warmed.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The Parkdale Agrimet site reported  10 inches of new snow between 20:00 PST on November 29th and 16:00 PST on November 30th. Other heavy snow reports were 11 inches of new snow reported at Middle Mountain RAWS and 8 inches of new snow reported by trained spotter HR-20 from Hood River Heights, Oregon.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong Pacific cold front brought strong southerly winds to the northern and central Oregon coast.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Strong winds, with sustained winds of 45 kt and gusts to 57 kt, were reported at Garibaldi NOS. Other strong winds reported were sustained winds of 43 kt with gusts to 54 kt at Cannon Beach, sustained winds of 43 kts at Clatsop Spit and gusts to 54 kts at Cape Meares.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Another Pacific frontal system brought strong winds to the northern and central Oregon coast.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Strong winds were reported at Clatsop Spit with sustained winds of 36 kts and at Garibaldi NOS with sustained winds of 39 kts.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Another Pacific frontal system brought strong winds to the northern and central Oregon coast.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Strong winds were reported at the Newport C-MAN station with sustained winds of 39 kts. Strong winds were also reported at Waldport and Yaquina Bridge with sustained winds of 39 kts and gusts to 50 kts.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong low pressure system dropped south out of British Columbia bringing cold air and heavy snow to the north Oregon Cascades and foothills, the Cascades in Lane County and the North Oregon Coast Range.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The Tombstone ODOT site measured 37 inches of new snow between 15:00 PST on November 21st and 22:00 PST on November 22nd. Other reports of heavy snow were 33 inches at the Santiam Pass ODOT site, 31 inches at the Blue Box Pass ODOT site, 28 inches at the Red Hill SNOTEL, 26 inches at the Mt. Hood Meadows Avalanche site, 25 inches at Little Meadows SNOTEL, 24 inches at the Jumpoff Joe SNOTEL, 23 inches at Government Camp and the Clackamas Lake SNOTEL sites, 22 inches at the Peavine Ridge SNOTEL and 21 inches at the Daly Lake SNOTEL.

EVENT_NARRATIVE: The Marion Forks SNOTEL site measured 23 inches of new snow between 15:00 PST on November 21st and 22:00 PST on November 22nd. The South Fork Bull Run SNOTEL site measured 20 inches of new snow between 12:00 PST on November 21st and 17:00 PST on November 22nd.

EVENT_NARRATIVE: The McKenzie SNOTEL site measured  33 inches of new snow between 14:00 PST on November 21st and 00:00 PST on November 23rd. Other reports of heavy snow were 33 inches at the Roaring River SNOTEL, 32 inches at the Cascade Summit SNOTEL, 26 inches at the Holland Meadows SNOTEL and 21 inches at the Summit Lake SNOTEL.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: The storm on the 20th through the 21st was the first in a parade of storms to hit Idaho and Oregon over a 5 day period. The heaviest snowfall with storm fell mainly in  Twin Falls County in South Central Idaho and Harney County in Southeast Oregon.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Spotters from Crane, Hines and Diamond reported 4 inches of new snow.

EVENT_NARRATIVE: The Sunset Summit ODOT site measured  10 inches of new snow between 14:00 PST on November 21st and 04:00 PST on November 23rd.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: An abormally cold air mass was in place over western Oregon with an easterly wind blowing through the Columbia River Gorge. The precipitation from an incoming Pacific frontal system fell through this air mass as freezing rain in the Gorge and Upper Hood River Valley causing travel to be treacherous.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Parkdale Agrimet reported 0.35 inch accumulation of ice as of 16:00 PST on November 26th. The Middle Mountain RAWS site reported 0.25 inch ice accumulation as of 12:00 PST on November 26th.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Moisture streaming northeast across the area combined with a tight surface pressure gradient produced blizzard conditions in the Grande Ronde Valley.  Visibility was 300 yards with roads drifted shut in the Island City, Cove, Union, and Ladd Canyon areas. Wind gusts over 50 mph were reported for several hours. ||Heavy snowfall also occurred.  Snowfall amounts in inches included Prairie City (10), 18 miles south southeast of Prairie City (6), Arbuckle SnoTel (10), 15 miles southeast of Heppner (12), 3 miles north northeast of Bingham Springs (14), Meacham (10.4), and High Ridge SnoTel (10).

EVENT_NARRATIVE: Hail up to walnut size reported.  Average size was between nickel and quarter.  Winds 30 to 40 mph accompanied the hail which completely covered the ground.

EVENT_NARRATIVE: Hail ranged from dime to nickel size and thinly coated the ground along Highway 206.

Snowfall amounts in inches included Camp Sherman (7), Clear Lake SnoTel (5), 2 miles southwest of Sisters (5.5), 5 miles west of LaPine (5), 4 miles west northwest of LaPine (12), and 3 miles north northeast of Sisters (5).

EVENT_NARRATIVE: Trained spotters around Harper and Juntura reported 6 to 7 inches of snow while spotters near Riverside and Jordan Valley reported 4 to 5 inches.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong Pacific cold front brought strong southerly winds to the central Oregon Coast.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Strong winds were reported at Yaquina Bridge with a sustained wind of 35 kts. Strong wind was also reported at Lincoln City with a gust of 50 kts.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong Pacific weather system brought strong winds to the northern and central Oregon Coast.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Strong winds were reported at the Garibaldi NOS site with sustained winds of 43 kts and gusts to 51 kts.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong Pacific weather system brought strong winds to the northern and central Oregon Coast.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Strong winds were reported on the Yaquina Bridge with sustained winds of 36 kts and gusts to 48 kts. Strong winds were also reported at the Newport C-MAN station with sustained winds of 38 kts and at Lincoln City with a gust to 57 kts.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A series of strong weather systems brought heavy rain to northwest Oregon causing flooding on local rivers.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Heavy rain caused the Luckiamute River to rise above flood stage.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A relatively weak, but tightly wrapped low pressure system moved into the far northeast Pacific ocean during the morning hours on January 13, 2010.  An occluded front associated with this system swept north across western Oregon, bringing some high winds to the central Oregon coast.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Amateur radio equipment in Gleneden Beach (located about 20 miles north of Newport, Oregon) measured a peak wind gust of 70 mph.||Additionally, the Oregon department of transportation equipment on the Yaquina Bay bridge in Newport, Oregon, measured sustained winds of 40 to 42mph.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A series of strong weather systems brought heavy rain to northwest Oregon causing flooding on local rivers.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Heavy rainfall over the area caused the Pudding River to overflow its banks and flood farmland.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Another in a string of winter storms moved through on this date. This one only had moderate snow amounts, but the snow level was low enough to impact areas that normally don't see much snow.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A member of the public reported 6 inches of roadside snow at 2700 feet between Williams and Applegate.  All this snow accumulated from early in the morning on the 26th until 26/1600 PST.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Yet another in a series of winter storms moved through on this date.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The COOP observer at Toketee Falls reported 8.0 inches of snow in 24 hours.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Yet another in a series of winter storms moved through on this date.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A Lake County low enforcement official deputy reported 6 inches of snow in 4 hours ending at 28/1900 PST.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A series of storms brought periods of heavy rain to the coast.  This led to a flood on the Coquille River as well as the South Fork of the Coquille River farther upstream.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The Coquille River at Coquille exceeded the flood stage of 21.00 feet at 29/0745 PST, crested at 23.64 feet at 30/0800 PST, then fell below flood state at 31/1615 PST.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A series of storms brought periods of heavy rain to the coast.  This led to a flood on the Coquille River as well as the South Fork of the Coquille River farther upstream.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The South Fork of the Coquille at Myrtle Point exceeded the flood stage of 33.00 feet at 29/1315 PST, crested at 33.12 feet at 29/1600 PST, then fell below flood stage at 29/1800 PST.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Yet another in a series of winter storms moved through on this date.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The COOP observer at Crater Lake National Park reported 30.0 inches of snow in 24 hours ending at 29/0800 PST.  The road in the park was closed for several days.  The avalanche danger had to be abated before the road could be cleared.  The cooperative observer at 3NNW Lemolo Lake reported 18.0 inches of snow in 24 hours ending at 29/1000 PST.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Yet another front brought strong winds to southwest and south central Oregon.  Several locations reported gusts in excess of 57 mph, but the strong gusts were short lived and only one occurred in an area where it would have a significant impact.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A spotter 2NE Valley Falls reported a wind gust to 59 mph at 18/0450 PST.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A series of systems moved into southwest Oregon during this time period.  Most of the precipitation fell as rain...but higher terrain in the area got heavy snow...especially in the Kalmiopsis Wilderness area.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The King Mountain SNOTEL, which is in zone ORZ023 but very close to ORZ024 at 4000 feet, recorded 11 inches of snow in 22 hours ending at 19/1200 PST, then another 11 inches in 23 hours ending at 20/1100 PST.  Warnings are not issued for 4000 feet altitudes in either zone, but the snow levels were considerably lower in ORZ024 with snow reported down to 1500 feet.  Therefore it is assumed that warning criteria was met above 2000 feet or so in the Kalmiopsis Wilderness area.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Another front brought marginal high winds to the coast.   The only reporting station that met criteria was Cape Blanco, which recorded a peak gust of 79 mph.  Usually Cape Blanco is not used to verify warnings, but this gust exceeded warning criteria by 21 mph and it is assumed that gusts to 58 mph occurred away from the Cape.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Another front brought marginal high winds to the coast. The only reporting station that met criteria was Cape Blanco, which recorded a peak gust of 79 mph. Usually Cape Blanco is not used to verify warnings, but this gust exceeded warning criteria by 21 mph and it is assumed that gusts to 58 mph occurred away from the Cape in northern zone ORZ022, which is just south of the Cape.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A cold winter storm with low snow levels moved through southwest Oregon and northern California on these days.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Based on rainfall amounts recorded at lower elevations in the area and a known snow level near 2500 feet, it is assumed that heavy snow fell at higher elevations in this zone.  Some 24-hour rainfall totals in the area were 1.14 inches at Agness, 1.40 inches at O'Brien, and 2.19 inches at Quail Prairie.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Another in a series of winter storms moved through the area. This one was associated with an arctic front...so snow fell at unusually low altitudes...even down to the coast.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Based on a number of reports of sufficient rain in this zone, including 2.20 inches at the Billie Creek Divide RAWS, 2.00 inches at the Cold Springs Camp RAWS, and 1.90 inches at Sevenmile Marsh RAWS, it is assumed that heavy snow fell at higher elevations.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A very cold air mass combined with clearing skies to make a cold night over southwest Oregon.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Reported low temperatures ranged from 31 to 38 degrees.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Another front brought marginal high winds to the coast.   The only reporting station that met criteria was Cape Blanco, which recorded a peak gust of 79 mph.  Usually Cape Blanco is not used to verify warnings, but this gust exceeded warning criteria by 21 mph and it is assumed that gusts to 58 mph occurred away from the Cape.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Another front brought marginal high winds to the coast. The only reporting station that met criteria was Cape Blanco, which recorded a peak gust of 79 mph. Usually Cape Blanco is not used to verify warnings, but this gust exceeded warning criteria by 21 mph and it is assumed that gusts to 58 mph occurred elsewhere in this zone away from the Cape.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A cold and wet system moved through the area...bringing heavy snow to most of northern California and parts of southwest and south central Oregon.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The snow in this zone occurred above populated areas.  Based on rainfall totals in the zone and a known snow level.  It is assumed that heavy snow fell above 2000 feet in this zone at the time the warning was in effect.

EVENT_NARRATIVE: Reports of 5 to 10 inches of snow were received from trained spotters.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A combination of light freezing rain and snow on the 4th of December caused roads in Baker County, Oregon to become slick and hazardous with many reports of accidents including one fatality.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Oregon State Police reported a commercial truck rollover on Interstate 84 near milepost 334 at approximately 11:22 AM MST resulting in one fatality and one injury. Multiple rollover and collision accidents as well as commercial truck jack- knife incidents were reported throughout the day.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A vigorous low pressure system moved northeast across parts of Eastern Oregon leaving heavy, wet snow over most of Harney and Malheur counties.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Six to 8 inches of new snow were reported by the Malheur County Sheriffs Department around Vale and Ontario.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: An avalanche, 200 feet wide,  buried and killed a snowmobiler from La Pine, Oregon on Paulina Peak which is south of Bend.
Lightning started the Cougar Creek Fire 17 miles southeast of Dayville on September 15th.  The fire burned 2100 acres through September 19th.

The first major winter storm of the season brought high winds to southern Oregon and northern California.  The strongest and most widespread winds occurred along the coast.  Strong winds also occurred at isolated locations inland at high altitudes and in areas where wind gets funnelled.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Wind gusts at the Flynn Prairie RAWS exceeded warning criteria from 23/2213 until 24/0013 PST.  The peak gust was 65 mph at 23/2213 PST.  A gust to 67 mph was recorded by a spotter at Nesika Beach at 23/2222 PST.  The AWOS at Gold Beach recorded a gust to 63 mph at 23/2239 PST.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: The first major winter storm of the season brought high winds to southern Oregon and northern California.  The strongest and most widespread winds occurred along the coast.  Strong winds also occurred at isolated locations inland at high altitudes and in areas where wind gets funnelled.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Wind gusts at the Summer Lake RAWS exceeded warning criteria from 24/0038 until 24/0338 PST.  The peak gust was 69 mph at 24/0338 PST.  A gust to 62 mph was recorded by a spotter 2NE Valley Falls at 24/0549 PST.  The RAWS at Summit recorded a gust to 61 mph at 24/1603 PST.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: The first major winter storm of the year generated heavy west to northwest swell...which resulted in high surf on the Oregon coast.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Offshore buoy data indicated that heavy swell impacted the southern Oregon coast.   The Umpqua Offshore buoy (46229) indicated high surf producing swell from 24/1138 PST until 25/1838 PST.  At the Port Orford buoy (46015), the high surf producing swell lasted from 24/1450 PST until 25/1650 PST. At the Point St. George buoy (46027), it lasted from 25/0950 PST until 26/1850 PST.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Thunderstorms moving through the Cascades in Lane County ignited a fire northeast of Mckenzie Bridge which burned 3464 acres.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The Scott Mountain Fire was started by lightning on Thursday, August 19, 2010. The fire burned 3464 acres in the Mt. Washington Wilderness and the National Forest managed by the McKenzie River Ranger District. The fire was contained by mid-September.

EVENT_NARRATIVE: Reported low temperatures ranged from 25 to 41 degrees.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: The first major winter storm of the season brought high winds to southern Oregon and northern California.  The strongest and most widespread winds occurred along the coast.  Strong winds also occurred at isolated locations inland at high altitudes and in areas where wind gets funnelled.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Winds at Cape Blanco met High Wind Warning criteria during this time interval with a maximum gust of 72 mph at 23/2010 PST.  Based on these observations, it is assumed that winds away from the capes reached an estimated 62 mph in other locations in the zone.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Another cold front brought strong winds to the southern Oregon coast.  This storm caused widespread power outages.  About 12000 Coos-Curry Electric customers were without power for up to 9 hours.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Flynn Prairie RAWS measured sustained south winds of 33-43 mph with gusts of 58-68 mph between 17/1213 PST and 17/2013 PST with a peak gust of 68 mph at 17/1613 PST. The Humbug Mountain ODOT station measured sustained southwest winds of 32-49 mph with gusts of 58-81 mph between 17/1534 PST and 17/2204 PST with a peak gust of 81 mph at 17/1734 PST. Lastly, the Red Mound RAWS measured a peak gust of 63 mph out of the southeast at 17/1321 PST.  A power outage lasting 8-12 hours occurred from Port Orford south to Brookings due to broken lines.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: An upper level trough over combined with a disturbance moving north from southeast Oregon brought severe thunderstorms to northeast Oregon during the late afternoon and evening.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Amateur radio operator reported 1 inch diameter hail.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: An upper level trough over combined with a disturbance moving north from southeast Oregon brought severe thunderstorms to northeast Oregon during the late afternoon and evening.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Hail of ping pong and golf ball size reported by the public and a spotter.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: An upper level trough over combined with a disturbance moving north from southeast Oregon brought severe thunderstorms to northeast Oregon during the late afternoon and evening.
The Rooster Rock Wildfire started August 2 at 10:44 am 6 miles south of Sisters.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The Rooster Rock Fire burned 6100 acres.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Strong southwest winds occurred ahead of a moderate cold front.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The Humbug Mountain ODOT station measured southwest winds of 35 mph with a peak gust of 58 mph at 01/0204 PST.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Strong southwest winds occurred ahead of a moderate cold front.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The Summer Lake RAWS measured southwest winds of 31 mph with a peak gust of 58 mph at 01/0038 PST.

CZ_NAME: NORTHERN & EASTERN KLAMATH COUNTY & WESTERN LAKE COUNTY
EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Yet another in a series of winter storms moved through on this date.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The COOP observer at Chemult reported 19.5 inches of snow in 24 hours.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong Fall storm brought the first heavy snow accumulations to the Oregon Cascades.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Snowfall estimates were reported to be 18 to 26 inches over the area, based on reports from the McKenzie River, Cascade Summit, Summit Lake, and Roaring River SNOTEL sites and the Willamette Pass ODOT site.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong Pacific frontal system brought strong winds to the northern and central Oregon coast.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Strong winds were reported on Highway 101 on the Yaquina Bridge in Newport. Strong wind was also reported at the Siuslaw Jetty in Florence with sustained wind of  39 kts.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Strong winds occurred ahead of a strong cold front along the Oregon coast and further inland across the ridges of Oregon.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Cape Blanco measured sustained south winds of 45 to 70 mph with gusts of 58 to 77 mph between 11/0540 PST and 11/1625 PST. The peak gust of 77 mph occurred at 11/1410 PST. While this site is not an official verification source, the assumption is being made that other areas in ORZ021 experienced winds gusting to 58 mph based on the high gust of 77 mph at Cape Blanco.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Strong winds occurred ahead of a strong cold front along the Oregon coast and further inland across the ridges of Oregon.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Flynn Prairie RAWS measured sustained south winds of 33 mph with a gust of 59 mph at 11/1813 PST. Meanwhile, the Humbug Mountain ODOT station measured sustained southwest winds of 30-44 mph with gusts of 60-61 mph between 12/0019 PST and 12/0127 PST. The peak gust of 61 mph occurred twice for Humbug Mountain at 12/0019 PST and 12/0119 PST.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Strong winds occurred ahead of a strong cold front along the Oregon coast and further inland across the ridges of Oregon.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Summer Lake RAWS measured sustained south-southwest winds of 23-28 mph with gusts of 62-64 mph between 11/2238 PST and 11/2328 PST. The peak gust of 64 mph occurred at 11/2338 PST.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Yet another in a series of winter storms moved through on this date.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The COOP observer at Prospect reported 7.0 inches of snow in 24 hours ending at 29/2000 PST. A CoCoRahs observer 7SW Prospect reported 8.5 inches of snow in 24 hours ending at 30/0900 PST.  A spotter 6SW Shady Cove reported 4.0 inches of snow in 9 hours ending at 29/2300 PST.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong Fall storm brought the first heavy snow accumulations to the Oregon Cascades.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Snowfall estimates were reported to be 18 to 31 inches over the area, based on reports from the Mount Hood Meadows Ski Resort, Mount Hood Test Site, Timberline Lodge, and the Hogg Pass and Red Hill SNOTEL sites.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Clearing skies and a cold air mass led to a freeze event for portions of the southern Oregon coast.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Reported low temperatures ranged from 29 to 32 degrees, except for a low of 34 degrees at the cooperative station near Gardiner.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Strong winds occurred along the southern Oregon coast ahead of strong cold front.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The Humbug Mountain ODOT weather station measured sustained southwesterly winds of 29-52 mph with gusts of 58-80 mph between 11/1506 PST and 11/1850 PST. The peak gust of 80 mph occurred at 11/1620 PST. Another verification site, the Flynn Prairie RAWS, measured a peak gust of 62 mph at 11/1613 PST. A fatality did occur as a result of the 30 foot waves offshore caused by the winds. A 45 year old male captain suffered a heart attack after abandoning a ship that was swamped by 30 foot waves|22 miles offshore from Crescent City, California on the 11th.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong cold pool of air aloft produced a very unstable airmass over western Oregon. |A vigorous low pressure center was just off the Washington Coast with a surface trough moving through western Oregon. This trough served as a focus for thunderstorms during the day. One of these thunderstorms produced a tornado that wreaked havoc on the community of Aumsville.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: On December 14th at 11:40 AM PST, a tornado with an Enhanced Fujita Scale rating of EF2 moved northeast across the community of Aumsville, Oregon. The estimated length of the tornado path was 5 miles with a width of 150 yards. There were two minor injuries, fifty structures damaged, more than thirty large trees blown down or snapped off, five sheds damaged and two semi-trailers tipped over. The estimated value of the damage was $1.2 million.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: The cold, unstable air mass following a strong Pacific weather system that moved through western Oregon produced a few thunderstorms. One of these thunderstorms produced a funnel cloud, hail and strong winds.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: On December 29th at 03:30 PM PST, a thunderstorm produced a funnel cloud, dime size hail and strong winds. A few trees and branches were blown down.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong cold pool of air aloft produced a very unstable airmass over western Oregon. A vigorous low pressure center was just off the Washington Coast with a surface trough moving through western Oregon. This trough served as a focus for thunderstorms during the day. These thunderstorms produced strong, gusty winds in several locations.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Strong, gusty winds were reported at Lincoln City with sustained winds of 35 kts and gusts to 53 kts.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong cold pool of air aloft produced a very unstable airmass over western Oregon. A vigorous low pressure center was just off the Washington Coast with a surface trough moving through western Oregon. This trough served as a focus for thunderstorms during the day. These thunderstorms produced strong, gusty winds in several locations.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Strong, gusty winds were reported at Clatsop Spit with sustained winds of 35 kts and gusts to 56 kts.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong cold pool of air aloft produced a very unstable airmass over western Oregon. A vigorous low pressure center was just off the Washington Coast with a surface trough moving through western Oregon. This trough served as a focus for thunderstorms during the day. These thunderstorms produced strong, gusty winds in several locations.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Gusty winds were reported at the Rockhouse RAWS site with gusts to 57 kts.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Strong winds occurred along the southern Oregon coast ahead of strong cold front.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The Port Orford weather station measured sustained southerly winds of 39-46 mph with gusts of 58-60 mph between 11/1606 PST and 11/1630 PST. The peak gust of 60 mph occurred at 11/1630 PST. Another gust of 60 mph was reported from a spotter 2SE Coos Bay at 11/1605 PST.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong Pacific weather system brought heavy rainfall to the area in mid December causing flooding on local rivers.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Heavy rainfall over the area caused the Pudding River to overtop its banks and flood farmland.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: The second part of the huge storm that hit Eastern Oregon and Southwest Idaho left another 12 to 18 inches of new snow across the region with 2 day totals of over 2 feet in some locations.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Trained spotters and coop observers reported from 6 to 18 inches of new snow.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: The second part of the huge storm that hit Eastern Oregon and Southwest Idaho left another 12 to 18 inches of new snow across the region with 2 day totals of over 2 feet in some locations.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Trained spotters reported from 6 to 12 inches of new snow and Snotels reported from 18 to 22 inches.

CZ_NAME: EAST SLOPES OF THE OREGON CASCADES
EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A moist southerly flow on December 28 was followed by a strong cold front on December 29.  Over a 48 hour period, the mountains above 4000 feet received over 2 feet of snow.  Reported snowfall amounts in inches included 5 miles north northwest of LaPine (12.5), 3 miles north northeast of Sisters (6), Camp Sherman (7), Seneca (7), Meacham (40), 9 miles north of Elgin (7.9), 10 miles east of Weston (12),5 miles southwest of Spout Springs (8), 6 miles southwest of Kamela (21), 10 miles north northeast of Ukiah (7), Tipton SnoTel (11), Lake Creek SnoTel (17), Sunriver (8.5), Arkbuckle Mtn. Snotel (15), 9 miles northwest of Seneca (7), 6 miles south southwest of Spout Springs (13), 7 miles west of Elgin (12), Flora (7.5), 13 miles northeast of Lostine (5), Spray Mountain (6), and 6 miles northwest of Flora (12).  The heavy wet snow caused some tree damage and power outages. In the Summerville area, power was out for up to 47 hours for 800 customers of Oregon Trail Electric Cooperative. Interstate 84 was closed between LaGrande and Pendleton the morning of December 29.  Three men and a 3 year old child were stranded and stuck in the snow overnight near Foreman Point on the Warm Springs Reservation.  The child and one of the men were treated for minor frostbite.

A cold easterly wind through the Columbia River Gorge was keeping cold air trapped in the Gorge as a strong Pacific frontal system moved inland. This system spread precipitation over the Gorge and Upper Hood River Valley, starting as snow and changing over to freezing rain in the Gorge as the air mass warmed.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The Cascade Locks observer reported 4 inches of new snow as of 07:00 PST on November 30th. A trained spotter, HR-19, in Hood River, Oregon reported 5 inches of new snow between 20:00 PST on November 29th and 10:00 PST on November 30th. Chief Meteorologist with KPTV, reported 1/2 inch of ice accumulation in Corbett as of 08:00 PST on November 30th.

CZ_NAME: EAST SLOPES OF THE OREGON CASCADES
EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A surge of moisture from the south brought heavy snowfall to much of the area from LaPine north to near The Dalles.  Snowfall reported in inches included Camp Sherman (9), 5 miles north northwest of LaPine (8), Sunriver (8), 3 miles southwest of Rowena (6), 9 miles northwest of Tumalo (7), 5 mles north northwest of Bend (6), 4 miles west northwest of LaPine (6), Redmond (8), 7 miles northwest of Madras (6.6), 5 miles south southwest of Chenowith (5.5), LaPine (6), Three Rivers (12), 5 miles north northwest of Redmond (7.6), and 9 miles northwest of Terrebonne (9).
A continued progressive weather pattern brought in another cold front which caused strong winds for the southern Oregon coast.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Flynn Prairie RAWS measured sustained southerly winds 40-51 mph with gusts of 59-78 mph between 18/2013 PST and 19/0013 PST. The peak gust of 78 mph occurred at 18/2113 PST. More storm reports came in as well with Gold Beach gusting to 66 mph at 18/2104 PST. Humbug Mountain had a peak gust of 78 mph between 18/2134 PST and 18/2149 PST. Red Mound RAWS measured a gust of 59 mph at 18/2221 PST. Finally, a local storm report from Brookings noted a 60 mph gust at 18/1839 PST.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong jet stream over the region kept the CWA under an unsettled pattern with a pacific cold front moving in causing strong winds along the south Oregon coast.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The Humbug Mountain ODOT station measured a southwest wind of 37 mph with a gust to 59 mph at 21/0104 PST. Even though the Cape Blanco station is not an official verification source, the station measured sustained south winds 35 to 59 mph with gusts of 59 to 67 mph between 20/0655 PST and 21/0010 PST which leads to further confidence that the warning verified for ORZ022.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A surge of moisture from the south brought heavy snowfall to much of the area from LaPine north to near The Dalles.  Snowfall reported in inches included Camp Sherman (9), 5 miles north northwest of LaPine (8), Sunriver (8), 3 miles southwest of Rowena (6), 9 miles northwest of Tumalo (7), 5 mles north northwest of Bend (6), 4 miles west northwest of LaPine (6), Redmond (8), 7 miles northwest of Madras (6.6), 5 miles south southwest of Chenowith (5.5), LaPine (6), Three Rivers (12), 5 miles north northwest of Redmond (7.6), and 9 miles northwest of Terrebonne (9).

A storm that moved into Eastern Oregon and Southwest Idaho in 2 parts on the 30th of November and the 1st of December left heavy snow in the Lower Treasure Valley with 4 to 7 inches of snow falling during the day on the 30th.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Malheur County Sheriff's office reported 6 inches of new snow had fallen as of 10 PM and a spotter reported 7 inches.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: An abormally cold air mass was in place over western Oregon with an easterly wind blowing through the Columbia River Gorge. The precipitation from an incoming Pacific frontal system fell through this air mass as freezing rain in the Gorge and Upper Hood River Valley causing travel to be treacherous.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Mark Nelson, with KPTV, reported that Corbett, Oregon received 1/4 inch of ice accumulation as of 09:00 PST on November 26th. The Department of Transportation closed Interstate 84 from Cascade Locks, Oregon to Hood River, Oregon for dangerous icy conditions on the roadway.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A cold front brought strong winds to the Oregon coast. Unfortunately, the strong winds occurred before the beginning of the warning period which resulted in a negative warning lead time.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Even though Cape Blanco is not an official verification source for ORZ021 it is hard to ignore the peak gust of 84 mph that occurred at 24/0325 PST. This severe gust lead to greater confidence that areas in ORZ021 gusted to at least 58 mph that morning as well. Sustained south winds of 40-74 mph with gusts of 58-84 mph occurred that day at Cape Blanco between 23/2340 PST and 24/1940 PST.

A cold front brought strong winds to the Oregon coast. Unfortunately, the strong winds occurred before the beginning of the warning period which resulted in a negative warning lead time.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The Flynn Prairie RAWS measured sustained south winds of 28-41 mph with gusts of 49-63 mph between 24/0413 PST and 24/1213 PST. The peak gust of 63 mph occurred at 24/1213 PST.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A continued progressive weather pattern brought in another cold front which caused strong winds for the southern Oregon coast.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Port Orford measured sustained southerly winds 35-60 mph with gusts of 58-76 mph between 18/2036 PST and 19/0036 PST. The peak gust of 76 mph occurred at 18/2112 PST. In addition, Cape Blanco measured sustained southerly winds of 87 mph at 18/2110 PST which further supports severe winds across ORZ021 even though Cape Blanco is not an official verification source. Fallen trees were reported in Elk River near Port Orford and the dock had minor damage from the boats hitting each other due to winds and waves.  There was also a gust to 76 mph recorded at the dock around 18/2100 PST.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Another cold front brought strong winds to the southern Oregon coast.  This storm caused widespread power outages.  About 12000 Coos-Curry Electric customers were without power for up to 9 hours.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Port Orford measured sustained south winds of 29-50 mph with gusts of 58-63 mph between 17/1218 PST and 17/1742 PST with a peak gust of 63 mph at 17/1618 PST and again at 17/1736 PST. The Long Prairie RAWS measured severe winds later in the day with a peak gust of 58 mph at 17/2213 PST. Even though Cape Blanco is not an official verification source, the impressive gust of 92 mph at 17/1510 PST is noteworthy.  Cape Arago recorded a gust to 70 mph at 17/2000 PST.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Another strong Pacific weather system brought strong southerly winds to the north Oregon Coast.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Strong winds were reported at the Garibaldi NWS observation site with sustained winds of 40 kts and gusts to 55 kts. Strong winds were also reported by a weather spotter at Cannon Beach with sustained winds of 35 kts with gusts to 52 kts.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Breezy conditions were present along the central Oregon coast for much of January 1, 2010, however a short period of high winds occurred during the afternoon hours.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The Oregon Department of Transportation site on the Yaquina Bay Bridge (located in Newport, Oregon) measured winds of 40 mph, with gusts to 50 mph at 12:04 pm PST.||Another measurement taken at Gleneden Beach (about 20 miles north of Yaquina Bay) measured sustained winds of 44 mph at 14:30 pm PST.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A cold front combined with a deep moisture southwest flow aloft to produce heavy snowfall across the Blue Mountains and Wallowa County. SnoTel (8), County Line SnoTel (6), Lucky Strike SnoTel (8), Beaver Reservoir SnoTel (7), Madison Butte SnoTel (6), Heppner-Spray Mtn Summit (10) and High Ridge SnoTel (8).

A very strong surface low moved into the far northeast Pacific ocean on the evening of January 18, 2010. As the occluded front associated with this system swept northward across western Oregon, high winds were recorded along the coast, and in the north Oregon Coast Range mountains.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The National Ocean Services equipment in Garibaldi measured sustained winds of 40 mph.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A very strong surface low moved into the far northeast Pacific ocean on the evening of January 18, 2010. As the occluded front associated with this system swept northward across western Oregon, high winds were recorded along the coast, and in the north Oregon Coast Range mountains.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Amateur radio equipment in Lincoln City recorded a peak gust of 62mph.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A very strong surface low moved into the far northeast Pacific ocean on the evening of January 18, 2010. As the occluded front associated with this system swept northward across western Oregon, high winds were recorded along the coast, and in the north Oregon Coast Range mountains.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Amateur radio operator in Forest Grove measured peak gusts in the 56 to 58 mph range. |While Forest Grove is technically just outside of ORZ003, the meteorological setup was such that the gusts recorded in Forest Grove can be used as a representative measurement for the nearby portions of ORZ003.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A series of lows moved through the northeast Pacific on the 17th and 18th, giving high winds to the north and central Oregon coast. These strong winds even stretched into the north Oregon Coast Range mountains.|Furthermore, gusty winds affected inland areas too, though winds there stayed below warning criteria.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: An amateur radio operator in Forest Grove, OR recorded a peak gust of 61mph.  Technically, Forest Grove is just outside of ORZ003 but, given the meteorological setup, can be used as a representative measurement of what was occurring in the north Oregon Coast Range mountains.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A relatively strong surface low moved into British Columbia on the morning of the 15th, draping a cold front just off the northwest Oregon coast. This front caused strong winds to occur along the north and central Oregon coast.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A national weather service trained spotter in Waldport, Oregon measured a wind gust of 72 mph.|Four other national weather service and amateur radio observations recorded sustained winds of 45-49mph, or wind gusts between 56 and 63mph.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A series of lows moved through the northeast Pacific on the 17th and 18th, giving high winds to the north and central Oregon coast. These strong winds even stretched into the north Oregon Coast Range mountains.|Furthermore, gusty winds affected inland areas too, though winds there stayed below warning criteria.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Garibaldi, Oregon measured a peak gust of 82mph, with sustained winds of 64mph.   Peak gusts of 67mph, 73mph, and 62mph were recorded at Clatsop Spit, Rockaway Beach, and Astoria respectively.  Downed trees in Tillamook caused some power outages as well.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A series of lows moved through the northeast Pacific on the 17th and 18th, giving high winds to the north and central Oregon coast. These strong winds even stretched into the north Oregon Coast Range mountains.|Furthermore, gusty winds affected inland areas too, though winds there stayed below warning criteria.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Equipment at an amateur radio site in Lincoln City recorded a peak wind gust of 79mph.  A trained spotter in Waldport measured a peak gust of 60mph.  Additionally, wind gusts of 72mph, 60mph and 63mph were recorded at three separate locations in Newport, while gusts to 64mph and 59mph were observed in Florence and Lincoln City respectively.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A quick moving low pressure system moved through the northeast Pacific on January 11, 2010.  The associated cold front caused some brief high winds along the north Oregon coast during the afternoon.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Clatsop Spit recorded sustained winds of 45 mph between 12:45pm and 15:30pm PST.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A relatively strong surface low moved into British Columbia on the morning of the 15th, draping a cold front just off the northwest Oregon coast. This front caused strong winds to occur along the north and central Oregon coast.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The National Weather Service equipment at Garibaldi, Oregon measured a peak gust of 64 mph. Additionally, Clatsop Spit measured sustained winds of 51 mph, with gusts up to 61 mph.  A spotter in Cannon Beach also reported wind gusts of 67 mph.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Strong south winds occurred ahead of a strong front, which brought severe winds to the south Oregon coast.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The Port Orford station measured south winds of 39-48 mph with gusts of 58-62 mph between 12/0248 PST and 12/0436 PST. The peak gust of 62 MPH occurred at 12/0330 PST.||The Flynn Prairie RAWS measured south winds of 37 mph with gusts of 58-61 mph between 11/2313 PST and 12/0213 PST. The peak gust of 66 mph occurred at 11/2313 PST.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Strong south winds occurred ahead of a strong front, which brought severe winds to the south Oregon coast.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The Humbug Mountain station measured southwest winds of 37-48 mph with gusts of 58-68 mph between 11/1843 PST and 11/2343 PST. The peak gust of 68 mph occurred at 11/1858 PST.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Very cold air behind a cold front brought snow levels down to as low as 2000 feet with significant snow accumulations.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The O'Brien station recorded 0.40 inches of precipitation between 09/1734 PST and 09/2334. Even using a wet snow ratio of 7:1 computes to 6 inches of snow, which is  meets Heavy Snow Warning criteria. Temperatures were 32-36F and the precipitation in the gauge could have been delayed until the snow melted later in the morning.||The Elk Valley HAAS station measured 0.52 inches of precipitation between 09/1700 PST and 10/1050 PST. The recording of this precipitation in the gage was delayed until temperatures rose above freezing. This fallen precipitation meets Heavy Snow Warning criteria of around 7 inches (4+ in 12 hours in Heavy Snow Warning).||The Agness RAWS measured 0.66 inches or precipitation between 09/1639 PST and 10/1139 PST with temperatures 31F to 37F. Again, the precipitation was delayed until temperatures warmed above freezing. This report is above Heavy Snow Warning criteria using an assumed wet snow ratio of 7:1.||Lastly, with delayed precipitation again, the Illinois Valley RAWS measured 0.42 inches of precipitation between 09/1652 PST and 10/0952 PST, which also meets criteria using an assumed snow ratio. Temperatures were between 31F and 45F.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Significant long period west-northwesterly swell entered the marine zones which created high surf (large breaker waves) along the coast.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Buoy 46229 (Umpqua Offshore) reported significant west-northwesterly swell heights of 20 feet at 17 seconds at 12/0008 PST. This was the longest swell period reported at this station. This swell extrapolates to 28 to 30 feet breakers at the coast, which meets High Surf Warning criteria.|Buoy 46015 (16 nautical miles west of Port Orford) reported significant west-northwesterly swell heights of 22.0 to 25.0 feet between 11/1700 PST and 12/0400 PST. The peak swell height of 25.0 feet occurred at 11/2000 PST. This swell had a swell period of 16.0 to 17.0 seconds during the same 11/1700 PST to 12/0400 PST time period. The longest period swell of 17.0 seconds occurred between 12/0200 PST and 12/0400 PST. This swell extrapolates to 33 to 35 feet breakers at the coast, which meets High Surf Warning criteria.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A cold front brought unexpected severe winds to the south Oregon coast. This was a missed event and the severe wind gusts occurred after the warning expired earlier in the morning.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Three severe wind gusts greater than 58 mph occurred during this missed event. The earliest gust of 59 mph occurred at the Flynn Prairie RAWS at 26/1513 PST. The Humbug Mountain ODOT weather station measured a 70 mph gust (the peak gust) at 26/1703 PST. A wind gust of 67 mph was reported by a spotter 1 mile southeast of Brookings at 26/1626 PST.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A series of strong weather systems brought heavy snow accumulations to the north Oregon Cascades and foothills and the Cascades in Lane County.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Timberline Lodge on Mt Hood measured 28 inches of new snow. Other reports of heavy snow were 26 inches at the Mount Hood Meadows Avalanche site, 22 inches at the Mount Hood Test Site and 21 inches at the Hogg Pass SNOTEL site.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A series of strong weather systems brought heavy snow accumulations to the north Oregon Cascades and foothills and the Cascades in Lane County.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A trained weather spotter from Zigzag, Oregon measured 7 inches of new snow.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A series of strong weather systems brought heavy snow accumulations to the north Oregon Cascades and foothills and the Cascades in Lane County.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The Cascade Summit SNOTEL site measured 30 inches of new snow. Other reports of heavy snow were 27 inches at the Summit Lake SNOTEL site, 21 inches at Hoodoo Ski Resort and 20 inches at the Holland Meadows SNOTEL site.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong Pacific cold front brought strong southerly winds to the northern and central Oregon coast.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Strong winds were reported at Lincoln City with gusts to 68 kts.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong low pressure system dropped south out of British Columbia bringing strong southerly winds to the northern and central Oregon coast.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Strong winds, with gusts to 52 kt, were reported in Lincoln City. Other strong winds were reported by the Waldport Spotter LC-73 with gusts to 50 kts.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong low pressure system moved through western Oregon bringing strong southerly winds to the north and central Oregon coast.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Strong winds were reported at the Yaquina Bridge ODOT site with sustained winds of 49 kts and gusts to 56 kts. Other strong winds were reported at the Newport C-MAN station with sustained winds of 39 kts and in Lincoln City with gusts to 52 kts.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Strong south to southwest winds occurred ahead of a strong cold front. Severe winds were experienced along the south Oregon coast.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The Humbug Mountain station measured sustained southwest winds of 34 to 49 mph with gusts of 59 to 73 mph between 02/0645 PST and 02/0815 PST. The peak gust of 73 mph occurred at 02/0729 PST. ||The Flynn Prairie RAWS measured a south wind of 36 mph with a gust to 60 mph at 02/0613 PST.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Strong southwest winds occurred ahead of a strong cold front. Severe winds were experienced across south central Oregon.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The Summer Lake RAWS measured a southwest wind of 29 mph with a gust to 59 mph at 02/0838 PST.||The Summit RAWS measured a southwest wind of 37 mph with a gust to 59 mph at 02/0903 PST. ||The peak gust for the entire zone of 69 mph occurred at 02/0910 PST 2 miles northeast of Valley Falls.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Significant subtropical moisture ahead of a strong cold front and strong cold air behind the front brought just the right synoptic conditions for heavy snow fall.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The Willamette Pass station (elevation 5080 feet) measured 1.20 inches of precipitation in a 9 hour period between 02/0447 PST and 02/1331 PST. Using a snow ratio of 10:1, produces 12 inches of snow with surface temperatures observed between 26F to 34F, which meets Heavy Snow Warning criteria.||Another report from ODOT on Highway 138 and Hwy 230 above 5000 feet measured 14 inches of snow between 02/1800 PST and 03/0000 PST.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A cold air mass settled over Oregon which brought well below minimum temperatures to the area under mostly clear skies at night.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Minimum temperatures ranged from 24 to 30 degrees.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Another night of well below normal minimum temperatures as a cold air mass continued over Oregon.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A minimum temperature of 28 degrees was observed.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A cold front brought a brief period of high winds to the north and central Oregon coast.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The National Ocean Services gauge at Garibaldi reported sustained winds of 40 knots, with gusts to 58 knots.|Also, National Weather Service equipment at Clatsop Spit reported sustained winds of 37 knots.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A cold front brought a brief period of high winds to the north and central Oregon coast.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Oregon Department of Transportation equipment on Yaquina Bay bridge measured sustained winds of 40 knots, with gusts to 58 knots.|Additionally, FAA equipment at Newport regional airport measured sustained winds of 36 knots, with gusts to 47 knots.  Other reports included wind speeds of 60 knot wind gusts at Cape Foulweather and 38 knots sustained winds at Gleneden Beach.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A weak occluded front brought high winds to the central Oregon coast for a couple of hours during the morning of thirteenth.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Oregon Department of Transportation equipment on the Yaquina Bay bridge in Newport, Oregon recorded sustained winds of 43 knots, with gusts to 56 knots.|Additionally, wind gusts of 48 knots were measured in Gleneden Beach, Oregon (which is about 20 miles to the north of Newport).

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Severe south to southwest winds occurred ahead of a strong cold front across south central Oregon.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The Coffee Pot Flat RAWS station measured sustained southwest winds of 29 to 34 mph with gusts of 59 to 61 mph between 29/1244 PST and 1744 PST. The Rock Creek RAWS station measured sustained southwest winds of 28 to 39 mph with gusts of 58 to 69 mph between 29/0344 PST and 29/1244 PST. The peak gust of 69 was the max gust for the entire zone which occurred at 29/0444 PST. Lastly, the Summer Lake RAWS measured a gust of 67 mph at 29/2038 PST. A 68 mph gust was reported at Valley Falls, Oregon at 29/0303 PST. A 70 mph gust was reported south-southwest Silver Lake, Oregon at 28/2139 PST. More reports in ORZ031 consisted of 68 mph again at Valley Falls, Oregon at 29/1116 PST and another gust at Silver Lake at 29/1355 PST of 65 mph.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Significant long period west-northwesterly swell entered the marine zones which created high surf (large breaker waves) along the coast.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Buoy 46220 (Umpqua offshore) measured 22 feet west-northwest swell with a period of 15 seconds at 03/0038 PST. This translates to 30 to 32 feet breakers at the coast which meets High Surf Warning criteria.||Buoy 26015 (16 nautical miles west of Port Orford) measured 22 to 24 feet west-northwest to northwest swell with a period of 14 to 16 seconds between 02/2100 PST and 03/0900 PST. This translates to 31 to 33 feet breakers at the coast which meets High Surf Warning criteria.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Significant long period west-northwesterly swell entered the marine zones which created high surf (large breaker waves) along the coast.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: This was a missed event as there was an advisory issued but no warning. Buoy 46027 (Pt. St George) measured 24 feet west-northwest swell with a period of 15 seconds  between 03/0300 PST and 03/0500 PST. This translates to 31 to 33 feet breakers at the coast which meets High Surf Warning criteria.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Strong south winds occurred ahead of a strong cold front which brought severe winds to the south Oregon coast.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Cape Blanco measured a 75 mph gust at 04/0840 PST and recorded gusts exceeding 60 mph from 04/0225 until 04/0840 PST. This is not an official verification source, however, due to the severity of the wind gust, we are making the assumption that the rest of ORZ021 saw at least wind gusts of 58 mph or higher during the morning and therefore the warning did verify.

CZ_NAME: NORTHERN & EASTERN KLAMATH COUNTY & WESTERN LAKE COUNTY
EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Severe south to southwest winds occurred ahead of a strong cold front across south central Oregon.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A gust of 60 mph occurred at Bonanza, Oregon at 29/1300 PST. There was roof damage reported to a hay shed with electrical line damage. A tree was reported down at the elementary school.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A cold air mass settled over Oregon which brought well below minimum temperatures to the area under mostly clear skies at night.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Minimum temperatures ranged from 27 to 31 degrees.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Strong south to southwest winds occurred ahead of a strong cold front. Severe winds were experienced along the south Oregon coast.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The Port Orford station measured sustained south winds of 39 to 51 mph with gusts of 49 to 61 mph between 02/0554 PST and 02/0712 PST. The peak gust of 61 mph occurred at 02/0712 PST. ||The Cape Arago station measured sustained south to southwest winds of 44 to 52 mph with gusts of 55 to 63 mph between 02/0600 PST and 02/0800 PST. The peak gust of 63 mph occurred at 02/0800 PST.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Very low snow levels in combination with moisture ahead of a cold front brought significant snow accumulations to the area.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Elk Valley (elevation 1705 feet) reported 0.88 inches of precipitation in a 17.5 hour period between 04/0930 PST and 05/0400 PST. Even using a wet snow ratio of 8:1, this comes out to 7 inches during this period which meets Heavy Snow Warning criteria.||O'Brien (elevation 1700 feet) reported 0.60 inches of precipitation during a 3 hour period between 04/1047 PST and 04/1432 PST. Using a 8:1 wet snow ratio, this comes out to 5 inches of snow during this 3 hour period, which meets Heavy Snow Warning criteria.||Finally, Agness (elevation 247 feet) reported 1.27 inches of precipitation in a 24 hour period from 04/0339 PST to 05/0339 PST. Temperatures were 35F to 47F so we are assuming that the snow that fell at higher elevations met Heavy Snow Warning criteria.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong cold front brought severe winds along the south Oregon Coast.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The Flynn Prairie RAWS weather station measured sustained south winds of 36 to 44 mph with gusts of 58 to 62 mph between 29/0213 PST and 29/0913 PST. The Humbug Mountain ODOT weather station measured sustained southwest winds of 35 to 52 mph with gusts of 58 to 83 mph between 29/0215 PST and 29/0829 PST.  The peak gust for the entire zone was 83 mph which occurred at the Humbug Mountain ODOT weather station at 29/0444 PST.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong cold front brought severe winds along the south Oregon Coast.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The Port Orford weather station measured a south wind of 43 mph with a gust to 60 mph at 29/0124 PST. The Cape Arago weather station measured a south wind of 43 mph with a gust to 60 mph at 29/0600 PST.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong cold front and upper level trough brought heavy snowfall to the northern Blue Mountains.  Snowfall amounts in inches included High Ridge SnoTel (9) and Tollgate (6).  Also a funnel cloud was observed near Echo.
Warm, moist, and unstable air flowing north ahead of negative tilted trough produced severe thunderstorms with large hail, high winds, and heavy rainfall across northeast Oregon.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Very heavy rainfall caused street flooding, ditch erosion, and water in some basements. One half to one inch of rain fell within a 20 minute period.  The very heavy rainfall limited visbility which caused an automobile-pedestrian accident with one injury.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A line of thunderstorms ahead of a fast moving cold front brought isolated high wind gusts to north central Oregon.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: North Pole Ridge RAWS site measured 59 mph gust with thunderstorm.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Unseasonably cold air behind a cold front brought very low temperatures to the area.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Minimum temperatures ranged from 27 to 28 degrees.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong cold front and upper level trough brought heavy snowfall to the northern Blue Mountains.  Snowfall amounts in inches included High Ridge SnoTel (9) and Tollgate (6).  Also a funnel cloud was observed near Echo.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Funnel cloud observed near Echo.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A tight pressure gradient combined with a cold frontal passage to produce widespread high winds and damage.  Blowing dust in the Columbia Basin reduced visibility to near zero around Stanfield, Pendleton, and between Lexington and Hermiston. The blowing dust caused traffic accidents with an injury near Stanfield on Interstate 84.  Peak wind gusts in mph included 13 miles west southwest of Mikkalo (58), 5 miles south southeast of Mikkalo (59), North Pole Ridge RAWS (65), Pendleton Airport (61), 10 miles north northeast of Pendleton (58), and  Helix (60).  Sustained winds of at least 40 mph were reported at 5 miles south southeast of Mikkalo.  Downed trees were observed 1 mile south of Stanfield, Blue Mountain Community College in Pendleton, 4 miles south of Bend, 12 miles south of Maupin, and Wasco.  A tree in the Bend area fell on a house and caused signficant structural damage.  Also in the Bend area, downed power lines caused small fires.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Severe winds occurred ahead of a strong cold front for south central Oregon.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong cold front moved across south central Oregon with severe winds occurring ahead of this front.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The Coffee Pot RAWS measured sustained south-southwest winds of 27 to 37 mph with gusts of 58 to 62 mph between between 26/1644 PST and 26/2144 PST. The peak gust of 62 mph occurred at 26/1644 PST. The Summer Lake station measured sustained southwest winds of 22 to 35 mph with gusts of 58 to 76 mph between 26/1638 PST and 27/0038 PST. The peak gust of 76 mph occurred at 26/1938 PST and again at 26/2138 PST. A spotter in Silver Lake had an estimated 65 mph gust at 26/1753 PST.  Another spotter in Silver Lake measured a gust of 77 mph at 26/2203 PST which was the maximum gust for the entire ORZ031 zone. Finally, a spotter 2 miles northeast of Valley Falls measured a 66 mph gust at 26/2330 PST.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A cold front combined with a strong jet stream aloft and tight surface pressure gradient produced high winds across central and northeast Oregon. Damage was reported in Condon, Enterprise, La Grande, Imbler, Elgin, Summerville, Camp Sherman, Lostine and three miles south southeast of Joseph.  Peak wind speeds measured in miles per hour include Ajax (62), Mikkalo (61), Patjens RAWS (62), 5 miles northeast of Sisters (71), 10 miles north northeast of Pendleton (58), Helix School (58), Fossil School (62), 9 miles east southeast of Stanfield (62), Pendleton Airport ASOS (67), North Pole Ridge RAWS (83), 3 miles north of Joseph (74), 8 miles west southwest of Echo (58).  Estimated wind speed of 60 mph was reported at Hermiston.

Other sub zero temperatures were observed at Bend (-8), Hermiston (-8), Joseph (-11), LaGrande (-6), Redmond (-8), Antelope (-5), Condon (-2), Moro (-7), Mitchell (-1), Seneca (-19), and Union (-8).

CZ_NAME: NORTHERN & EASTERN KLAMATH COUNTY & WESTERN LAKE COUNTY
EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Locally heavy snow occurred in south central Oregon due to an ongoing storm.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The cooperative observer at Chemult reported 7.1 inches of snow in 13 hours.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Locally heavy snow occurred in south central Oregon due to an ongoing storm.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A SNOTEL observation platform at Dismal Swamp recorded 7 inches of snow in 11 hours.  Six inches fell in the first seven hours.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: An incoming cold front brought heavy precipitation to portions of southwest Oregon with a snow level of around 4500 feet.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: An incoming cold front brought heavy precipitation to portions of southwest Oregon, but due to high snow levels...around 4500 feet...no reports of high snow were received.  However, heavy rain reports were recorded in Josephine county, including 1.90 inches 2SW O'Brien, 1.90 inches 1SSW of O'Brien, and 1.38 inches 3.7W of Cave Junction, all  in 24 hours.  The extreme western tip of ORZ028 is near these stations, so based on these reports, it is estimated that heavy snow occurred at higher elevations in zone ORZ028.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong cold front brought high winds to the southern Oregon Coast and to portions of Lake county east of the Cascades.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: None of the reporting stations that are normally used to verify warnings reported high winds with this system.  However, the Cape Blanco station reported gusts as high as 75 mph (at 07/1310 PST) and six gusts 65 mph or greater.  Based on these observations, it is assumed that high winds occurred away from the point in zone ORZ021.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong cold front brought high winds to the southern Oregon Coast and to portions of Lake county east of the Cascades.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: None of the reporting stations that are normally used to verify warnings reported high winds with this system.  However, the Cape Blanco station reported gusts as high as 75 mph (at 07/1310 PST) and six gusts 65 mph or greater.  Based on these observations, it is assumed that high winds occurred just south of the point in zone ORZ022.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong cold front brought high winds to the southern Oregon Coast and to portions of Lake county east of the Cascades.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The Coffee Pot Flat RAWS reported a gust to 58 mph at 08/0144 PST.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong Pacific frontal system brought strong winds to the northern and central Oregon coast.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Strong winds, with sustained wind s of 38 kt and gusts to 52 kts, were reported at Garibaldi NWS. Other strong sustained winds reported include 39 kt at Garibaldi NOS and 41 kt at Clatsop Spit.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong line of thunderstorms moved across the central portion of western Oregon generating strong winds and hail and causing some tree damage.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A thunderstorm produced strong winds at Springfield and Eugene, Oregon with gusts to 51 kt.  A tree was also blown down by strong winds near Oakridge, OR.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong line of thunderstorms moved across the central portion of western Oregon generating strong winds and hail and causing some tree damage.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A thunderstorm produced strong winds in Waldport with a gust of 52 kt.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: An incoming cold front brought high winds to areas of south central Oregon.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A spotter 2SSW Valley Falls reported a gust to 60 mph at 14/0450 PST.  The Summer Lake RAWS recorded a gust to 62 mph at 14/0830 PST and a gust to 70 mph at 14/1030 PST.

CZ_NAME: EAST SLOPES OF THE OREGON CASCADES
EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A moist westerly flow brought heavy snowfall to northwest Deschutes County. Snowfall amounts in inches were 3 miles southwest of Sisters (7), 3 miles north northeast of Sisters (9).
An incoming cold front brought high winds to areas of south central Oregon.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A large tree fell on a vehicle parked on a back road, killing the person asleep inside.  Reported wind gusts on the ridges were 42-53 mph, while valley wind gusts peaked at 20-35 mph.  The sheriff's deputy at the scene said the tree was healthy.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A very cold air mass combined with clearing skies to make a cold night over southwest Oregon.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Reported low temperatures ranged from 28 to 30 degrees, except for Langlois, which reported a low of 37 degrees.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Southerly flow aloft ahead of an eastern Pacific upper level low pressure and a strong surface pressure gradient produced high winds.  Peak wind gusts in mph included Hot Lake (61) and 3 miles north of Elgin (58).

The Oak Flat wildfire started around 10 miles northwest of Selma Oregon around 0830 PDT on 08/13/2010. The cause is under investigation, but it is likely that it was human caused. The fire was declared 100% contained on 09/08/2010. The fire area was 7494 acres and 18.4 million dollars had been expended on the fire. No structures were lost.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The Oak Flat wildfire started around 10 miles northwest of Selma Oregon around 0830 PDT on 08/13/2010. The cause is under investigation, but it is likely that it was human caused. The fire was declared 100% contained on 09/08/2010. The fire area was 7494 acres and 18.4 million dollars had been expended on the fire.  No structures were lost.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A fire sparked by a transient consumed a barn and grassland on the west side of Interstate 5 near the south Ashland exit.  Embers from the fire blew across the interstate and set a number of homes on fire east of the interstate.  In the end, 11 homes were destroyed and three more were damaged.  No deaths or injuries were reported, but the neighborhood was evacuated and the interstate was closed.  Weather conditions that day was very hot with a high of 98 recorded in Ashland with relative humidities in the single digits.  Winds were upvalley and fairly typical for this time of year and this time of day.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Weather conditions were hot and dry at the time of the fire.  Ashland recorded a high temperature of 98 degrees with relative humidities in the single digits.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: The Oak Flat wildfire started around 10 miles northwest of Selma Oregon around 0830 PDT on 08/13/2010.  The cause is under investigation, but it is likely that it was human caused.  As of 09/01/2010, the fire area was 6515 acres and 70 percent contained.  14.5 million dollars has been expended on the fire.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The Oak Flat wildfire started around 10 miles northwest of Selma Oregon around 0830 PDT on 08/13/2010.  The cause is under investigation, but it is likely that it was human caused.  As of 09/01/2010, the fire area was 6515 acres and 70 percent contained.  14.5 million dollars has been expended on the fire.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Heavy rainfall in early June pushed many streams and rivers to near or above flood stage.  Flooding occurred on Little Creek in Union, Mill Creek in Cove, Imnaha River, John Day River, Wallowa River, and Grand Ronde River.  A landslide was reported along the Lostine River near Turkey Flats.
An upper level trough combined with daytime heat to enhance instability and produce thunderstorms over Union and Grant counties.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Up to quarter sized hail covered the ground to 1 inch depth.  Power outage occurred.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Hot and moist air at low levels combined with an upper level disturbance to spawn many thunderstorms over southwest Oregon on this date.  The winds were not strong...but heavy rain and large hail created problems across the area.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: This spotter also reported 0.80 inches of rain.
